summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs a shell pipeline via execFileSync('/bin/sh','-c',...) that harvests /proc/1/environ, full process environment, AWS IMDSv2 instance role credentials, AWS Secrets Manager contents across regions, and recursively greps for HTB{...} flag strings under /home/node, /app, and /root. The collected data is HTTP PUT in chunks to hardcoded endpoints http://127.0.0.1:30782 and http://154.57.164.71:30782 (TARGETS array at postinstall.js:6). Execution is gated to hosts whose hostname matches /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/ — the default Docker container hostname shape — indicating the package is designed to fire inside containerized CI / CTF / cloud build environments. The package has no legitimate functionality: index.js exports {}, metadata has no author/repo/homepage, and the postinstall is the only behavior. Any container that runs `npm install sea-bound-siren` will have its AWS role credentials and environment secrets shipped to 154.57.164.71:30782.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
